feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud, connectivity
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud, connectivity Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to get a list of Users by License used in the system
entry.content:
Good afternoon Partners

Will anyone know how I can obtain a list of the users who are logged into my system and what license they are occupying?

The idea is that I can click and it will show me the users who are logged in with the types of licenses.

I tried with the Activity_Log Model but it does not show me the requested information.

Does anyone know how I can resolve this issue?

Thank you!!!!

Greetings

entry.title: Dropdown filtering in SAC
entry.content:
SAP Managed Tags: SAP Analytics Cloud, analytics designer

Hi community

I am new to SAC.

I want to introduce a dropdown filtering menu in my story. I succeded in populating the dropdown using these lines of code in the onInitialization() function : 


var Inspectors_list = Table_1.getDataSource().getMembers("Don_ordre.Inspecteur");

if (Inspectors_list.length > 0) 
{
for (var i=0;i<Inspectors_list.length; i++){
Dropdown_1.addItem(Inspectors_list[i].id,Inspectors_list[i].description);
}}
 
Then i proceeded to to adding the following lines of code in the Dropdown_1 - onSelect() :

var x = Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();
console.log(x);
Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Don_ordre.Inspecteur",x);


The filter is not working correctly and i do not understand why.
There is only two values in the dropdown once selected returns values , all the others returns zero.

Also i figured if it's an issue with my model , why would two values from the dropdown filter correctly and the others won't.

 

 

Also some screenshots from the modeler

entry.title: SAC Story need to transport from non-prod(DEV) to SAC Prod with datasphere live connection
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I know there is a way we can do the connection change at model for other data sources scenario.

But here DataSource is datasphere in live which we don't have a model for that to change a model connection at preferences.

If we change a non-prod imported story model to prod connection model, then the entire format will change.

Do you guys have any ideas about this approach?

entry.title: DM_SCHEDULE not displaying the Job End time
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have some import jobs to load fact data in SAC Planning model. We are also monitoring the jobs last run via DM_SCHEDULE model report. This model provided by SAP and it helps to monitor the Data management jobs. It's showing Null for Job end time and job start time. It worked before the SAC Q3 Upgrade. Anyone facing this issue ?

entry.title: Inbound delivery
entry.content:
Hi Experts

While doing post goods receipt I am facing error that is import clearance message is not received from customs Authority

Can anyone help me to resolve this issue

entry.title: Number formatting changes when executing SAC Stories in View Mode or via Hyperlink
entry.content:
Problem:

The Number formatting changes when the story is executed in view mode or story is executed via Hyperlink or story is executed from the link shared.

Workaround :

The story works fine when we access directly from the Public folder. We are using Live connection and Backend is SAP HANA. 

Impact :

We have created a Dashboard with all the story Hyperlinks and Business will access the respective Dashboards from the Hyperlink provided. From the Business point of view nothing is working as expected.

Observation :  

The issue started with latest SAC patch upgrade on Aug 17-18th 2024.

Currently we are with this version :

Versions

2024.15.8 (Client)

2024.15.6 (Server)

Build Dates

Wed Aug 28 2024 16:36:34+0000 (Client)

Wed Aug 21 2024 17:52:03+0200 (Server)

Few more Observations from tests conducted :

  1. Number formatting changes for a newly created story as well when used measure based input control.
  2. Number formatting changes even when there is no % calculations involved.
  3. Number formatting changes only with the Cross calculations.
  4. Above issues exists in Optimized design for both Responsive page and Classic page as well.
  5. Only few story have this issue after the SAC Patch upgrade.

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Analytics Cloud for planning #SACCROSSCALCULATIONS #SACPATCH

entry.title: FreeHand SQL query with input parameters
entry.content:
Good morning SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere experts.

I recently created a successful OData connection from a view with Datasphere parameters to use the data in an SAP Analytics Cloud Planning model.

The problem I have is that I want to select the parameters from a FreeHand query when creating the model and I get different errors.

I am testing several syntaxes:

CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP(P_MANDT='100',P_DATE_FROM=1980-01-01,P_DATE_TO=1980-01-01)/Set

And I get this error:

If I use this syntax

CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP/CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP(P_MANDT='100',P_DATE_FROM=1980-01-01,P_DATE_TO=1980-01-01)/Set

I get an error like this:

When I use this syntax:

CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP(P_MANDT='100',P_DATE_FROM=1980-01-01,P_DATE_TO=1980-01-01)

I get this error:

So I understand that the syntax here is incorrect.

I have tried ending the statement with /Results but it doesn't work either.

I have consulted other questions and documentation such as these:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sac-how-to-use-odata-v4-with-parameters-exposed-from-a-s-4hana-cds-view/qaq-p/12690021
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-sac-free-hand-query/qaq-p/12622748
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/bwplaolap/3361384392.html

But I have not been able to find a solution

What would I have to do to be able to do the freehand query for views with input parameters coming from Datasphere over OData connections?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP analytics cloud imputcontrol date-type
entry.content: Hello, I have some InputControls with date-type data. I need a button to remove them, but InputControl only allows me to modify members that are of type string.

entry.title: Unable to retrieve data from OData API for Reports from SAP Business ByDesing to SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

Data is missing when importing data from SAP Business ByDesign Analytics in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

This issue is happening only for a few reports created on top of a join data source FINGLAU04 (Financial Statement) and CRMCIVIB (Customer Invoice Item) for example.

Connection name: CC_HOME_ANALYTICS

Connection description: CC_Home_Analytics

We are using a business user with all work centers authorisation access and rights. 

Connection works for most of the data sources exposed in ByD and standard and custom reports. 

Data is missing when importing data from SAP Business ByDesign Analytics in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

Has anyone experienced the same issue trying to retrieve data from FINGLAU04 Financial Statement (Basic Data Source) from Business ByDesign into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)?

Kind regards,

Sergio

entry.title: SACAgentKit Cloud Agent Connector
entry.content:
Can 1 Cloud Connector & Agent be used to connect BW Test to SAC Test, and BW Prod to SAC Prod?

This is for importing data to SAC Planning.

entry.title: How to integrate Workforce analytics in SAP SAC
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

Can any one guide us to create a connection from Success factor workforce analytics to SAP Analytics Cloud.
we would required to know the configurations in Success factor to enable workforce analytics.

Expecting a quick support.

Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: SAC Add-in file in SAC Environment, transport Dev to quality it should automatically change the URL?
entry.content: Add-in file is saved in SAC and when i am trying to transport it from Dev environment to Quality URL doesn't change?

entry.title: Always login HANA via Live Connection in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
My company currently using SAC connect to SAP HANA database (on-premise, not HANA cloud). I want to provide about 50 SAC account for 50 Sale Employee in order to view their sales report. The problem is I use a Live Connection, so I must do Data Authorization in HANA. So for each SAC account there is a corresponding HANA account to authorize. I've tried 2 Direct Live Connection: Username Password and SSO. But both of them need to login HANA again every time I restart my laptop or shut down and power on again (for Username Password there is a Set Credential pop-up when access to the story, and for SSO I need to go <host>:<port> to login the HANA account again. But I don't want to provide my sale employees HANA account, It's very sensitive. I just want to go to their device and login HANA once. The questions are: 
1. Is there another solution for my case beside Username Password and SSO?
2. Is there any way to keep the login of Username Password to HANA in SAC, or keep always login in <host>:<port> for SSO after every time I restart the devices?
Thank you !

entry.title: SAC Live Connection to SAP HANA using SSO auto Logon
entry.content:
Hi, currently using SSO Authentication for my Live Connection from SAC to SAP HANA. Everything work perfectly except the one thing: The SAP HANA XS auto log off after I restart my computer or after a day, and I need to log in again( image below). How can I config HANA XS never log out (session time out = never) ? Because I share the report for other 50 people in my company, and each people has his/her own SAC account corresponding to a HANA account (because I use a Live Connection so the only way to data authentication is in HANA instead of in SAC). And I don't want to provide password of HANA to user because of sensitive so I want to setup the logon HANA XS once for each user, so that every time they view the report, the logon session still there.

Thank you

entry.title: SAC SSO Dynamic User Creatinon
entry.content:
Hello experts,

 I have configured SSO to SAC (IAS as Proxy and Azure as IdP). In SAC, I enabled ‘Dynamic User Creation’ and set the "User Attribute" = email. When I try to verify my user in SAC under Step 4 against Azure AD, I can log in in SAC, but it seems that the user attributes are not being transmitted. It looks like this, see screenshot.

 

Also, I don’t see the "SAML USER MAPPING" column under Users in SAC.

Why are the user attributes not being transmitted, and how can I enable the "SAML USER MAPPING" column?

Thank you very much !!!

Best Regards

entry.title: SAC Tunnel Live Connection SAP HANA error
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I tried to make a Live Connection from SAC to SAP HANA using Tunnel (Protocol type is TCP SSL) but I got the error "You are not authorized to query the remote system. Please ask your administrator to grant you the InA role" (image 1).
But in HANA I have set the InA permission for my account (image 2).
Then I use Chrome Developer tools to track the error to "https://<tenant> .analytics.cloud.sap/lcs/scc/tunnel/tunnel/sap/bc/ina/service/v2/GetServerInfo"  and got the error: "The connection could not be retrieved. connectionName: tunnel remotePath: /sap/bc/ina/service/v2/GetServerInfo" (image 3)
Thank you
Regards.
Thanh Hieu

entry.title: SAC Hyperlinking with BW Live connection
entry.content:
Hello

I'm trying to achieve hyperlinking in SAC with model based on BW live connection. 

senario1 : External URL 

From story1 trying to pass only selected dimension but story2 has no effect on click of dimension(G/L Account) but if I select any measure it will pass all the dimensions in row as filter .

 

senario2 : Story linking with apply selected dimension as filter;(Story1 Screenshot attached ) 

this doesn't show any link(screenshot dimension1) while clicking on dimensions but on click of measure it filters out all the dimensions o Row. 

story2 screenshot attached (measure with all dimensions on row) .

could you please suggest how to achieve Report to Report linking on click of dimension values in SAC based on BW live connection? Is it a product limitation ?what could be the work around for this ?

entry.title: Import Connection with odataV4 between S4hana to SAC
entry.content:
Hello experts,
We use an on-premis system and want to connect the SAC via an import data connection. I have found the documentation “Import Data Connection to SAP S/4HANA”: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/63140f17362947fe8bcd9c6960db23bc.html?locale=en-US&q=Odata
At the beginning it says that only odata V2 is supported, is that really the case? There is no mention of this here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/4c002a6b954d43f18f3712cb10ac3cdc.html?locale=en-US#concept_mv1_ztv_ktb__section_psv_13w_ktb

If I want to import my data model via this interface, only the published OdataV2 serives are displayed in the SAC. Do I have to change anything in the settings?
Greetings Niels

entry.title: Is it possible to connect SAP Analytics Cloud to Google BigQuery using a service account?
entry.content:
I am currently using a trial version of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and I am trying to connect it to Google BigQuery. I have successfully established a connection using a personal account, but I haven't seen an option to use a service account for authentication.

Any guidance or insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Álvaro

entry.title: SAC - "You do not have access to edit this job. Contact the connection owner"
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In SAP Analytics Cloud I was importing the Business Content model called "Rapid Financial Planning & Analysis for SAP S/4HANA (SAP Best Practices)". Then I edited the S/4HANA connection pointing to my own S4 by changing the URL and the user and password. When I want to import data into the model I get the following message "You do not have access to edit this job. Contact the connection owner "XXXX" and ask them to give you access to "SAP S/4HANA". The strange thing is that the owner of the connection is my own user.

A few weeks ago the connection had been imported by another user, but to avoid problems, I deleted it and imported it under my own user.

What is the missing permission?

Greetings & Regards
